Please do not send post directly to the constituency office as it will take at least two days longer to reach me. . Please do not come to my constituency office in person without an appointment; my team is not able to see people who have not booked an appointment in advance. Please send all letters to: Yasmin Qureshi MP House of Commons LONDON SW1A 0AA Packages from courier companies should addressed to Yasmin Qureshi MP c/o CEVA Logistics, 40-48 Chase Road, London, NW10 6PX. . '> View on Google Maps Telephone 01204 371202 Email qureshiy2@parliament.uk If you want to contact me about any issue, please include your full name and full postal address in your letter or email. If you have casework or issues, the best way to contact me is via the webform here: https://members.parliament.uk/member/3924/contact The constituency office telephone number is 01204 371202 . Please do not come to my constituency office in person without an appointment – my team and I are not able to see people who do not have an appointment booked in advance. I am able to help constituents with the following issues: Tax problems involving the HM Revenue and Customs Department Welfare issues dealt with by the Department for Work and Pensions such as benefits, tax credits and state pensions Issues handled by the Home Office such as immigration and passports (but I cannot give immigration advice, see below) Issues relating to the Department of Health and Social Care such as concerns about hospitals or access to treatment Any issues arising from any other government departments Issues with utility companies Consumer issues I can support constituents by: Writing to the Minister with responsibility for the department concerned Asking an official for an explanation of why a decision has been made Asking for a decision to be reviewed What I can’t help with: Please note that I am not legally permitted to give immigration advice. For all immigration advice, you should speak to a qualified legal adviser. I am not able to countersign photographs for passports or driving licences. I am not able to provide references for people whom I have not known well for at least two years. My office is not presently able to offer work experience, shadowing or internships. If you wish to report an immigration crime, such as someone living or working in the UK illegally, employing someone who isn’t allowed to work in the UK, involved in smuggling or involved in illegal immigration, you must fill in the online form here: https://www.gov.uk/report-immigration-crime Asking an MP to report it on your behalf will only slow down the progress of the report and create unnecessary duplication. Please see the advice section of my website for further information on immigration casework . Early Day Motions (EDMs) I don’t usually sign EDMs. Whilst they may bring an issue to public attention, they rarely have a meaningful effect and there are numerous other ways of effectively raising an issue.
